summary: Do not ship: three post-r9 structural gaps remain despite the valid pinned RED→GREEN pair.

findings.title: Blocker: exhaustive pagination is re-bounded by spawnSync
findings.body: `runSoft` buffers the complete output of `gh api graphql --paginate --slurp`. The Node-compatible `spawnSync` contract has a finite output buffer—Node documents a 1 MiB default—and this code supplies neither streaming nor `maxBuffer`. Same-head fork PRs can therefore exceed the subprocess boundary, kill `gh`, and repeatedly suppress the targeted app's polling. This recreates the fork-saturation primitive below the GraphQL pagination layer. See the [Node child-process contract](https://nodejs.org/api/child_process.html).
findings.recommendation: Fetch GraphQL pages incrementally with a bounded per-page subprocess or streaming API, reducing each page immediately instead of using `--slurp`. Add a witness whose aggregate response exceeds the default subprocess buffer.

findings.title: Blocker: reconcile treats an unobservable author as “not ours”
findings.body: The sibling parser accepts an omitted `author` field as `null`, while the main parser correctly treats omission as schema failure. `isTrustedSibling` then filters the PR out, so an armed writer PR can disappear from reconciliation and the run exits successfully without `revocationBlind`. This violates V-2's requirement that observation failures capable of hiding revocation subjects fail loudly and exposes the cost of maintaining two PR parsers/trust models.
findings.recommendation: Use one canonical PR observation parser and trust predicate. Require the `author` field to be present, allow only explicit `null` as the deleted-account state, and aggregate missing/malformed authorship as a revocation observation failure.

findings.title: Blocker: the branch escapes scope[] and publishes the retired seam
findings.body: `tools/README.md` is changed against main but is absent from the lock's three-path `scope[]`, violating the stated single-flip rule. Its new public contract is also materially obsolete: it advertises bounded `gh pr list` discovery and `BEHIND → skip`, while the implementation uses exhaustive GraphQL and rebuilds BEHIND. The increment artifact remains similarly stale at `docs/increments/bump-poll-duplicate-pr/B-1-idempotent-pr.md:19`.
findings.recommendation: Bring the README into the declared scope and rebuild the frozen RED/GREEN pair with the corrected contract, or revert it into a separate scoped documentation change. Synchronize the plan and increment artifact with the final GraphQL, BEHIND, reconcile-only, and 106/63 contracts.

next_steps: Replace buffered slurping with bounded incremental pagination.
Unify PR parsing and add the missing-author revocation witness.
Resolve the scope/documentation drift, then regenerate machine-owned verify records without changing tests between pins.
